judgment.summary: Workday demonstrates robust earnings growth and strong cash flow generation at a highly attractive valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 2.47 beat the 2.32 estimate (6.4% surprise); next earnings date is 2026-05-21.
positives: PEG ratio 0.48 — suggests the stock is undervalued relative to its growth.
Forward P/E 10.04 vs trailing 48.27 — indicates significant expected earnings expansion.
Earnings growth 62.9% — shows strong bottom-line momentum.
Free cash flow (TTM) $3.01B — provides substantial liquidity for the business.
negatives: Profit margin 7.3% — represents relatively thin net profitability.
Return on assets 3.6% — indicates low efficiency in asset utilization.
Operating margin 12.0% — shows moderate operational efficiency.

news.paragraph: Workday shares have recently plummeted alongside other software stocks, though the company is also seeing developments related to new AI integrations and the launch of its Solopreneur Accelerator. Traders should weigh this recent price volatility against the potential impact of the company's Microsoft 365 Copilot integration on its enterprise AI moat. No specific upcoming earnings or regulatory catalysts are explicitly identified in the recent headlines.
